sourceMovie.title: Stranger Than Fiction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Stranger Than Fiction for the same reason it broke through in 2006: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Marc Forster keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ruby Sparks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stranger Than Fiction clicked for you, Ruby Sparks (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Stranger Than Fiction?
faq.answer: Try Ruby Sparks, Defending Your Life, Love, Rosie—each shares DNA with Stranger Than Fiction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Stranger Than Fiction?
faq.answer: Stranger Than Fiction (2006) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Romance, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Stranger Than Fiction worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
